skill.name: amazon-listing-competitor-analysis-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users analyze Amazon competitor listings by ASIN and produce structured competitive intelligence plus strategic opportunity points for their own go-to-market. The Agent should proactively apply this skill when users want to analyze a competitor Amazon listing by ASIN, understand what a top-ranked product does right in content keywords or visuals, find market gaps and unmet buyer needs, turn competitor research into opportunity maps for their brand, identify keyword placement patterns on rival listings, extract SEO insights from Amazon product pages, reverse-engineer competitor bullet and title strategies, mine competitor reviews for buyer psychology, compare seller and A plus content patterns, run gap analysis before launching a new SKU, research why a listing wins conversion signals, synthesize whitespace you can own versus the diagnosed listing, or say just look at this ASIN with a competitive or optimization angle.
skill.summary: Analyze Amazon competitor listings by ASIN with structured competitive intelligence and strategic opportunity mapping.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 85 potential issues with a 100/100 risk score. After semantic evaluation, these are predominantly false positives: network calls go to legitimate BrowserAct API (api.browseract.com), environment access is standard API key retrieval, and external command references are documentation examples in markdown. The skill is a straightforward wrapper around BrowserAct's Amazon listing extraction service with no malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Python Script Execution via Command Line
medium_findings.description: The skill invokes Python scripts via command-line arguments. Script accepts ASIN and marketplace URL as parameters. Arguments are passed from user input without shell expansion.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Script execution is documented in SKILL.md. Arguments are passed directly to Python without shell expansion. No command injection vectors identified.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables. This is standard practice for API credential management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable access is standard API key pattern. No credential exfiltration detected - key is used only for BrowserAct API authentication.

low_findings.title: Network Access to BrowserAct API
low_findings.description: Script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com for Amazon listing extraction. All network calls are to a single, legitimate third-party service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls target a single known API endpoint. No data exfiltration detected - network access is the core functionality of this skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Flags
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged system reconnaissance at lines 45-53 of python script. These are JSON response parsing checks (checking for 'id', 'Invalid authorization', 'concurrent' in API responses).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching incorrectly flagged JSON parsing as system reconnaissance. Code is checking API response structure, not scanning the user's system.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flags
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithms at multiple SKILL.md locations. Analysis shows these are word matches in documentation (e.g., 'verify', 'encrypt', 'hash', 'encode' in usage instructions) with no actual cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic functions found in code. Static analyzer is flagging common words in documentation context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution Flags
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick execution in SKILL.md. Analysis shows these are markdown code block examples (documentation showing python -u ./scripts/... commands), not actual shell executions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matching incorrectly flagged documentation code examples as shell command executions. No backtick or shell expansion detected in actual Python code.

content.user_title: Analyze Amazon competitor listings by ASIN
content.value_statement: Stop spending hours manually researching competitor listings. This skill extracts structured data from any Amazon product page and generates actionable competitive intelligence for your brand positioning.
actual_capabilities: Extract full product data from any Amazon listing using ASIN
Diagnose competitor content strategy including title formula and bullet priority
Map keyword placement across title, bullets, description, and A+ content
Analyze visual content strategy from extracted image and video data
Identify market gaps and unmet buyer needs from review analysis
Generate strategic opportunity points for your brand positioning
limitations: Requires BrowserAct API key for execution
Execution involves polling latency (can take several minutes for extraction)
Analysis quality depends on data completeness from Amazon listing

use_cases.title: Competitor Teardown for New Product Launch
use_cases.description: Before launching a new SKU, analyze top-ranked competitors to understand their title formula, keyword strategy, and visual approach. Use insights to position your listing for differentiated search visibility.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers and brand owners preparing marketplace launches

use_cases.title: Organic Rank Gap Analysis
use_cases.description: Map which keywords a competitor ranks for in their title versus bullets versus description. Identify underserved search intents where you can capture traffic they are missing.
use_cases.target_user: SEO specialists and Amazon advertisers optimizing organic reach

use_cases.title: Review-Driven Product Development
use_cases.description: Extract recurring buyer complaints and wishes from competitor reviews to inform product improvements, bundle strategies, or feature roadmaps based on real market demand.
use_cases.target_user: Product development teams and category managers

prompt_templates.title: Basic competitor analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze competitor ASIN B0CS62LY6P on Amazon.com. Give me a full breakdown of what they do well and where the market has gaps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick analysis of a single competitor listing

prompt_templates.title: Multi-marketplace research
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the same competitor analysis for ASIN B0CS62LY6P on Amazon.de and Amazon.co.uk to compare keyword strategies across European marketplaces.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-launch competitive positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: I am launching a similar product in the kitchen accessories category. Analyze top competitors by ASIN and identify whitespace I can own in my positioning and keyword strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword gap analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the keyword placement strategy from competitor ASIN XXXXXXX. Tell me which high-traffic terms they rank for in their title versus bullets versus description, and what search intents they are missing.

output_examples.input: Analyze competitor ASIN B0CS62LY6P on Amazon.com
output_examples.output:
Competitor ASIN: B0CS62LY6P | Brand: ExampleBrand | BSR: 1234 | Rating: 4.5 (892 reviews)

WHAT THIS COMPETITOR DOES RIGHT
Content Strategy:
- Title formula: Brand-first with primary keyword at position 2
- Bullet priority: Lead with durability, follow with ease-of-use
- Standout phrasing: Uses 'military-grade' for durability positioning

MARKET GAPS
- Content gap: Under-serves 'easy cleanup' use case despite positive review mentions
- Keyword gap: 'Dishwasher safe' appears in reviews but not in copy

YOUR STRATEGIC OPPORTUNITY POINTS
- Claim the 'easy maintenance' angle they under-own
- Target 'dishwasher safe' long-tail where they are weak

output_examples.input: Compare keyword strategy for ASIN B0CS62LY6P vs ASIN B09XYZ123
output_examples.output:
ASIN B0CS62LY6P leads with durability in title (first 80 chars). ASIN B09XYZ123 leads with use-case. 

B0CS62LY6P owns 'heavy duty' and 'stainless steel' in primary position. B09XYZ123 owns 'portable' and 'lightweight' in title.

Gap identified: Neither competitor owns 'compact' + 'heavy duty' combination - potential positioning opening for differentiator.

output_examples.input: Find unmet buyer needs from competitor reviews
output_examples.output:
From ASIN B0CS62LY6P 3-star reviews, recurring themes:
- 'Good but too small for family use' (mentioned 12 times)
- 'Wished it had variable speed control' (mentioned 8 times)
- 'Instructions hard to follow' (mentioned 6 times)

Opportunity: Develop a family-sized variant or bundle with speed control dial. Create better illustrated quick-start guide for differentiation.

best_practices: Confirm your BrowserAct API key is set before running analysis
Use extracted data as single source of truth - do not invent listing facts
Run multiple competitors for the same category to build comprehensive market view
anti_patterns: Do not prompt for competitor analysis without verifying API key availability first
Do not blend competitor findings with your own listing recommendations - keep analysis and opportunity synthesis separate
Do not ask Claude to optimize the competitor listing - the skill generates your strategic opportunities, not edits to their page

faq.question: How do I get started with this skill?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable or provide it when prompted. Get your key from browseract.com/reception/integrations.

faq.question: What parameters does the skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill takes an ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) and optional marketplace URL. Default marketplace is Amazon.com. Example: python ./scripts/amazon_listing_competitor_analysis.py B0CS62LY6P

faq.question: How long does the analysis take?
faq.answer: Phase 1 extraction involves automated browser operations and typically takes 2-5 minutes depending on listing complexity and Amazon page load times. Phase 2 analysis generates immediately after extraction completes.

faq.question: What marketplaces does this support?
faq.answer: Any Amazon regional marketplace including .com, .co.uk, .de, .fr, .it, .es, .ca, .co.jp, and .com.mx. Specify the marketplace URL as the second parameter when running the script.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script detects invalid authorization and halts with an error message directing you to check your BrowserAct API key. It will not retry with bad credentials.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual competitor research?
faq.answer: Extraction produces structured data in seconds versus manual copying. The Phase 2 framework systematizes analysis across content, keywords, visuals, and buyer signals that would take hours to compile manually.
